prompt.title: Scale CRM — Automation Agents System (Addendum to MVP)
prompt.scope: This file defines the full Automation Agents system for Scale CRM. It extends the base platform (scale_replit_prompt.json v1). Implement all four agent configuration modules under /admin/agents/ as dedicated full-page sections, not tabs. Each agent is a complete configuration surface with its own sub-navigation.
prompt.design_rules_inherited: All design rules from v1 apply: no gradients on UI, no glows, no shadows on cards, Inter font, #1A1A3E navy text, #2B62E8 accent, 1px #E4E4E8 borders, white cards on #F7F7F8 bg, 8px border-radius max, font-weight max 600. This addendum adds no new design tokens.

routing_structure.agents_index: /admin/agents — overview page listing all 4 agents with status + quick stats
routing_structure.shared_sidebar_sub_nav: Under 'AI Agents' in the main sidebar, show sub-items: Overview, Follow-Up, Chat, Order Tracking, Refund. Indent 8px with a connecting line on left.

agents_overview_page.description: Four agent cards in a 2x2 grid. Each card shows: agent name, enable/disable toggle, 3 key live stats (last 7 days), last edited timestamp, 'Configure' primary link. A global 'Human Intervention Alerts' button top-right opens the notifications settings panel.

agent_pages.name: Lead Follow-Up Agent
agent_pages.description: The follow-up agent contacts new leads automatically across WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook. This page is its full control center: LLM personality, follow-up sequences, timing rules, channel config, triggers, and escalation settings.
agent_pages.page_layout: Three-column on desktop: left nav (sticky, 200px, section anchors), center content (max 720px, scrollable), right summary panel (240px, live status + enable toggle).

right_panel.description: Sticky summary card. Shows: Agent status toggle (on/off), 'Last triggered: X mins ago', Active sequences count, a 'Test agent' button that opens a simulate modal where admin types a fake lead message and sees what the agent would send back step by step.
right_panel.test_modal: Admin inputs: contact name, channel (select), opening message. On submit: show the full sequence the agent would run — each step on a timeline with the actual rendered message text, delays shown between steps, and a note if the sequence would stop at any point.

sections.title: LLM Configuration
sections.description: Configure the underlying language model that generates follow-up messages.

fields.label: AI provider
fields.help: The LLM provider that generates message content.

fields.label: API key
fields.help: Stored encrypted. Never displayed after save.
actions: Test connection button — sends a test ping and shows 'Connected' or error inline

fields.label: Model
fields.help: Faster/cheaper models for follow-up sequences are usually fine.

fields.label: Creativity
fields.help: Lower = more predictable messages. For follow-ups, keep below 0.5.

fields.label: Max message length
fields.help: Maximum tokens per generated message. WhatsApp messages should stay short — recommended 120–200.

sections.title: Agent personality
sections.description: Define how the agent sounds. This becomes part of the system prompt.

fields.label: Agent name
fields.placeholder: e.g. Sara
fields.help: The name the agent introduces itself as. Used in {{agent_name}} variable.

options.label: Direct
options.desc: Short, no fluff, gets to the point

fields.label: Primary language
fields.help: The agent will generate messages in this language by default.

fields.label: System prompt
fields.placeholder: You are Sara, a friendly sales assistant for [Company]. You follow up with leads who expressed interest in our products. Keep messages short, natural, and focused on understanding what the customer needs. Never be pushy. Always end with a question to keep the conversation going.
fields.help: This is sent to the LLM before every message. Be specific about what the business sells, who the customer is, and what the agent should and should not say.

fields.label: Never discuss
fields.placeholder: Add topic and press Enter
fields.help: Topics the agent must never bring up or respond to. If mentioned by customer, agent escalates to human.

fields.label: Business context
fields.placeholder: We sell handmade leather bags. Our customers are mostly 25–45 year old women. Average order value is 3,500 DZD. We operate in Algiers and ship nationwide.
fields.help: Background information the LLM uses to make messages relevant. The more specific, the better the output.

sections.title: Follow-up sequence
sections.description: Define the exact steps the agent runs after a new lead comes in. Each step is a message sent at a specific delay. The sequence stops the moment the lead replies.
sections.ui: Vertical timeline of step cards. Each card draggable to reorder (drag handle on left). 'Add step' button at bottom.

step_card_fields.label: Send after
step_card_fields.description: Two inputs: number + unit select (minutes / hours / days). First step can be 0 (immediate).

options.label: Fixed template
options.desc: Use an approved message template. Required for WhatsApp first contact.

options.label: AI generated
options.desc: LLM writes the message using the system prompt and conversation context.

options.label: Template + AI personalization
options.desc: Start with a template, let AI personalize the last sentence.

step_card_fields.label: Template
step_card_fields.description: Pick from approved templates. Shows channel compatibility icons.

step_card_fields.label: Message instruction
step_card_fields.placeholder: Remind the lead about the product they asked about. Mention that stock is limited. End with: 'Can I answer any questions for you?'
step_card_fields.help: Step-specific instruction for the LLM. Combined with the system prompt.

step_card_fields.label: Send on
step_card_fields.help: If the lead came in via Instagram, only Instagram will be used regardless of selection.

step_card_fields.label: Only send between
step_card_fields.help: If delay puts the send time outside this window, it shifts to the next valid time.

step_card_fields.label: Stop this step if
options: Lead replied to any previous step
Lead was manually marked as Contacted
Agent has taken over the conversation

fields.label: Max steps before stopping
fields.help: Even if lead never replies, stop after this many messages.

fields.label: Restart if lead re-engages after 30 days
Global send window
fields.help: Overrides per-step windows if set.

sections.title: Triggers
sections.description: Define exactly when the follow-up sequence starts.

options.label: New lead added from any channel
New lead added via WhatsApp only
New lead added via Instagram only
New lead added via Facebook only
Lead moves to a specific stage
Agent manually triggers from lead record
A specific tag is added to the lead

fields.label: Tag to trigger on
fields.placeholder: e.g. hot-lead, re-engage

fields.label: Do not trigger if
options: Lead already has an active sequence
Lead was contacted in the last 24 hours
Lead is assigned to an agent who has paused automation
Lead is marked as Unsubscribed or Blocked

sections.title: Human intervention
sections.description: Define when the agent stops and a human agent is notified.

options.label: Customer uses a complaint keyword (define below)
LLM confidence is low — question outside scope
Customer explicitly asks for a human or agent
Lead deal value exceeds threshold (define below)
Full sequence completed with no reply

fields.label: Complaint keywords
fields.placeholder: Add keyword
fields.help: Any of these in a customer message will immediately pause the agent and alert a human.

fields.label: Notify
fields.help: Which team members receive the urgent notification when escalation triggers.

fields.label: Notification message template
fields.placeholder: URGENT: {{contact_name}} needs human help. Reason: {{escalation_reason}}. Open: {{conversation_link}}
fields.help: Variables: {{contact_name}}, {{escalation_reason}}, {{conversation_link}}, {{agent_name}}, {{channel}}

fields.label: Pause agent after escalation
fields.help: If off, the agent continues sending the next scheduled step even during escalation.

agent_pages.name: Client Chat Agent
agent_pages.description: The chat agent handles all inbound customer messages 24/7 — answering questions, qualifying leads, and handing off to humans when needed. This page configures its full behavior: LLM model, personality, knowledge base (FAQ), response rules, mood settings, and escalation logic.
agent_pages.page_layout: Same three-column layout as follow-up agent. Right panel shows: live status, messages handled today, current active conversations count, 'Test chat' button.
agent_pages.test_modal: Chat simulator. Admin types messages as if they were a customer, agent replies in real time using the actual configured LLM + system prompt. Shows confidence score per reply (internal, not sent to customer). Useful for testing edge cases before going live.

sections.title: LLM configuration
sections.description: Same structure as follow-up agent LLM config — provider, API key, model, temperature, max tokens. Separate keys per agent so different models can be used.

additional_fields.label: Max response time
additional_fields.help: If LLM takes longer than this, send a 'Give me a moment' holding message automatically.

additional_fields.label: Holding message
additional_fields.placeholder: Just a moment, let me check that for you! 🙏
additional_fields.help: Sent immediately if LLM response takes longer than timeout.

sections.title: Personality & mood
sections.description: Define the agent's voice, tone, and emotional style. This is more detailed for the chat agent since it handles real-time conversations.

fields.label: Agent name
fields.placeholder: Sara
fields.help: Name shown in conversation and used in responses.

fields.label: Avatar initial
fields.help: Single letter shown in the chat interface for the agent.

options.label: Friendly
options.desc: Warm, uses light emojis, casual phrasing

fields.label: Adapt mood to customer
fields.help: If on, the agent mirrors the customer's energy level — more formal if they're formal, warmer if they're casual.

fields.label: System prompt
fields.placeholder: You are Sara, a customer support assistant for [Company Name]. You help customers with questions about our products, pricing, availability, and delivery. You are friendly but professional. Always answer accurately — if you don't know something, say so and offer to connect the customer with a team member. Never make promises about delivery times or prices unless they are in your knowledge base. Keep answers concise — 1–3 sentences unless more detail is needed.
fields.help: Core instructions for the LLM. This runs before every single message. Be specific.

fields.label: Greeting message
fields.placeholder: Hi! 👋 I'm Sara, your assistant. How can I help you today?
fields.help: Sent automatically when a customer opens a conversation for the first time.

fields.label: Business context
fields.placeholder: We are [Company]. We sell [products]. Operating hours: Mon–Sat 9am–6pm. Delivery: 2–5 days nationwide. Payment: CCP, Baridimob, cash on delivery.
fields.help: Facts the agent should always have access to. Injected into every conversation context.

fields.label: Never discuss
fields.placeholder: Add topic

sections.title: Knowledge base (FAQ)
sections.description: The agent's primary source of answers. When a customer asks something, the agent first checks the knowledge base before using the LLM to generate a free-form answer.
sections.ui: Table with add/edit inline. Two columns: Question and Answer. Each row: question text, answer text (truncated to 60 chars + expand), channel tags, edit icon, delete icon. 'Add entry' button opens inline row editor.

fields_per_entry.label: Answer
fields_per_entry.placeholder: We deliver within 2–5 business days across Algeria. Algiers and Oran: 1–2 days.

fields_per_entry.label: Match keywords
fields_per_entry.help: If any of these appear in a customer message, this entry is considered a match. Leave empty to rely on semantic similarity.

fields.label: Similarity threshold
fields.help: How closely a customer message must match a FAQ entry to use it. Lower = more lenient matching.

fields.label: Use LLM if no match found
fields.help: If off, agent sends a fallback message and escalates when nothing matches.

fields.label: No-match fallback message
fields.placeholder: I don't have that information on hand. Let me connect you with a team member.

sections.title: Response rules
sections.description: Define specific guardrails and behaviors for how the agent responds.

fields.label: Max consecutive AI messages
fields.help: If agent sends this many messages without a customer reply, escalate to human.

fields.label: When customer sends an image or file
options: Acknowledge only: 'Thanks, a team member will review this'

fields.label: After hours
options: Send an after-hours message + queue for morning

fields.label: After-hours message
fields.placeholder: We're currently closed. We'll get back to you first thing tomorrow morning! 🌙

fields.label: Business hours
fields.help: Set operating hours. Agent respects these for after-hours behavior.

fields.label: Auto-qualify leads
fields.help: If on, the agent asks qualifying questions (defined below) and moves the lead stage based on answers.

fields.label: Qualifying questions
fields.placeholder:
What product are you interested in?
What's your approximate budget?
Is this for personal use or business?
fields.help: Agent weaves these into the conversation naturally — not as a form. Order matters.

sections.title: Triggers
sections.description: When the chat agent activates and how it handles different incoming message types.

fields.label: Agent responds to
options: All inbound messages from new contacts
All inbound messages from existing leads
Only messages on conversations not owned by a human agent

fields.label: Instant-response keywords
fields.placeholder: Add keyword
fields.help: When these appear in a message, skip FAQ matching and respond immediately with LLM.

fields.label: Move lead to stage when customer replies

sections.title: Human intervention
sections.description: When the chat agent stops and escalates to a human agent. Same structure as follow-up agent but with chat-specific triggers.

fields.label: Escalate when
options: Customer explicitly asks for a human
Customer sends an image or document
Customer asks about refund or return
Customer mentions a specific order number
Conversation idle for more than X minutes after agent message

fields.label: LLM confidence threshold
fields.help: Below this score, the reply is flagged as uncertain and escalated.

fields.label: Escalate if customer doesn't reply after
Notification text
fields.placeholder: ⚡ Human needed: {{contact_name}} on {{channel}} — {{escalation_reason}}

agent_pages.name: Order Tracking Agent
agent_pages.description: The tracking agent connects to a delivery API, monitors order statuses, and sends customers proactive updates. When a customer is unsatisfied or a delivery fails, it triggers the Refund Agent and notifies the business owner. This page configures the delivery API connection, update message templates, trigger conditions, and the handoff to the refund flow.
agent_pages.page_layout: Three-column. Right panel: delivery API connection status dot (green/red), orders being tracked today, failed deliveries today, 'Test API connection' button.

sections.title: Delivery API connection
sections.description: Connect to your delivery partner's API. The agent polls this API to get live status updates and triggers customer messages when status changes.

fields.label: Delivery partner
fields.help: Selecting a preset auto-fills the API base URL and authentication method.

fields.label: API base URL
fields.placeholder: https://api.yalidine.app/v1/
fields.help: Auto-filled for presets. Edit only for custom integrations.

fields.label: API key / Token
fields.placeholder: Your delivery API key
actions: Test connection — shows 'Connected' with last sync time or error message with code

fields.label: API ID / Client ID
fields.placeholder: Required by some providers (e.g. Yalidine)
fields.help: Some APIs require both an ID and a key. Check your delivery partner's documentation.

options.label: Webhook (real-time)
options.desc: Delivery API pushes updates to Scale. Fastest. Requires delivery partner to support webhooks.

options.label: Polling (scheduled)
options.desc: Scale checks the API every X minutes. More compatible, slight delay.

fields.label: Your webhook endpoint
fields.help: Paste this into your delivery partner's webhook settings.

fields.label: Order ID field name in API
fields.placeholder: tracking_code
fields.help: The field name in the API response that contains the order/tracking ID. Used to match delivery API records to Scale leads.

fields.label: Order ID stored in Scale as
fields.help: Where in Scale the order ID is stored so the agent can match orders to contacts.

sections.title: Status mapping & customer messages
sections.description: Map each delivery status from the API to a customer-facing message. The agent sends the message to the customer when that status is detected.
sections.ui: Table with rows per status. Each row: API status code/name (left, readonly label) + customer message textarea (right, editable) + send toggle (enable/disable per status) + channel selector. 'Add custom status' link at bottom.

default_statuses.default_message: Hi {{name}}! ✅ Your order has been confirmed. We're preparing it now. Your tracking code is: {{tracking_code}}
Good news {{name}}! Your order is being packed and will be handed to our delivery partner soon.
{{name}}, your order is on the move! 🚚 Our delivery partner has picked it up. Expected: {{estimated_delivery}}
Your order is out for delivery today {{name}}! Please make sure someone is available to receive it. 📦
Your order has been delivered {{name}}! 🎉 We hope you love it. If you have any questions, just reply here.
Hi {{name}}, our delivery team tried to reach you today but couldn't. They'll try again tomorrow — please make sure you're available.
Hi {{name}}, your order was returned to us. This sometimes happens if delivery was attempted multiple times. A team member will contact you to reschedule.
We're very sorry {{name}} — there seems to be an issue with your delivery. A team member will contact you within 1 hour to resolve this.

fields.label: Default send channel
fields.help: Use the same channel the lead originally came from.

fields.label: Don't send same status update twice

sections.title: Satisfaction detection
sections.description: After a delivery update is sent, the agent monitors the customer's reply for satisfaction signals. If the customer is unhappy, the Refund Agent is triggered and the owner is notified.

fields.label: Monitor customer replies after delivery
fields.help: If on, the chat agent reads the customer's reply to the delivery update and classifies it as satisfied, neutral, or unsatisfied.

fields.label: Satisfaction check message
fields.placeholder: Hi {{name}}, we hope everything arrived perfectly! 😊 Are you happy with your order?
fields.help: Sent 1–2 hours after the 'delivered' status is confirmed. Optional — leave blank to skip.

fields.label: Unsatisfied signal keywords
fields.help: Any of these in a reply after delivery = unsatisfied customer. Triggers refund agent.

fields.label: When customer is unsatisfied
options: Send empathy message first, then trigger Refund Agent
default: Send empathy message first, then trigger Refund Agent

fields.label: Empathy message
fields.placeholder: We're really sorry to hear that {{name}}. This is not the experience we want for you. Our team is already looking into this and will get back to you shortly.
fields.condition: unsatisfied_action includes Send empathy message first

fields.label: Auto-link orders to leads
fields.help: When a new order is created, automatically match it to a lead by phone number or order ID.

fields.label: If order can't be matched to a lead
Wait before sending update
fields.help: Delay between API status change detection and customer message. Use 0 for immediate.

sections.title: Human intervention & owner notifications
sections.description: The order tracking agent notifies humans in two cases: failed deliveries and unsatisfied customers.

fields.label: Notify business owner when
options: Order marked as 'returned' by delivery partner
Order marked as 'lost' by delivery partner
Delivery is delayed beyond estimated date by 2+ days

fields.label: Owner notification recipients
fields.help: These users receive the owner-level notification. Typically: Business Owner + Admin.

fields.label: Owner notification message
fields.help: Variables: {{tracking_code}}, {{contact_name}}, {{delivery_status}}, {{issue_description}}, {{conversation_link}}, {{order_value}}

fields.label: Notify assigned sales agent when
options: Customer is unsatisfied and needs follow-up
Delivery failed and refund flow started
Customer replies outside of tracking context

fields.label: Auto-trigger Refund Agent on dissatisfaction
fields.help: Immediately hands the conversation to the Refund Agent when dissatisfaction is detected.

agent_pages.name: Refund Agent
agent_pages.description: The refund agent takes over when a customer is unsatisfied — after being triggered by the Order Tracking Agent or by a direct customer request. It handles the full refund conversation: acknowledging the issue, collecting the reason, checking policy, deciding auto-approve or escalate, and notifying the owner for any case that needs discussion. This page configures the full refund logic, LLM personality, policy rules, escalation flow, and owner notification templates.
agent_pages.page_layout: Three-column. Right panel: refund requests today, auto-approved today, pending owner review, 'View all active refunds' link.

sections.title: LLM configuration
sections.description: Same structure as other agents. Refund conversations are sensitive — recommend a higher-quality model (GPT-4o or Claude Sonnet) and lower temperature.

additional_fields.content: For refund conversations, set creativity to 0.2 or lower. The agent must be predictable and policy-compliant. Use a strong model.

sections.title: Personality & tone
sections.description: The refund agent must be empathetic and clear. It is handling an unhappy customer.

fields.label: Agent name
fields.placeholder: Sara

options.label: Empathetic + Professional
options.desc: Starts warm, shifts to clear process

fields.label: System prompt
fields.placeholder: You are Sara, handling refund and return requests for [Company]. A customer has reached out because they are unhappy with their order. Your job is to: (1) Sincerely apologize for the inconvenience, (2) Understand exactly what the issue is by asking one clear question, (3) Check if the request qualifies for a refund based on our policy, (4) If it qualifies, confirm the refund and explain next steps clearly, (5) If it doesn't qualify or you're unsure, tell the customer a manager will review and get back to them within [timeframe]. Never argue with the customer. Never promise things you cannot guarantee. Be honest about the process.
fields.help: This prompt is critical. Be specific about what the agent can and cannot promise.

fields.label: Opening message when triggered
fields.placeholder: Hi {{name}}, I'm really sorry you had this experience. I'm Sara, and I'm here to make this right. Can you tell me a bit more about what happened with your order?
fields.help: Sent as the first message when the Refund Agent takes over from Order Tracking.

fields.label: Refund context
fields.placeholder: Our standard refund policy: customers have 7 days from delivery to request a refund. Items must be unused and in original packaging. Damaged items are refunded without return. Wrong items sent are refunded or replaced at no cost.
fields.help: The agent uses this as ground truth for policy decisions.

sections.title: Refund policy
sections.description: Define the rules the agent uses to automatically approve or escalate a refund request.

fields.label: Refund window
fields.help: Requests made after this window are automatically escalated, not auto-approved.

fields.label: Auto-approve limit
fields.help: Refund requests below this amount are auto-approved if policy conditions are met.

fields.label: Require proof for damaged/wrong items
fields.help: If on, agent asks customer for a photo before approving.

fields.label: Proof request message
fields.placeholder: To process your refund quickly, could you send me a photo of the item? That really helps us resolve this faster. 🙏

fields.label: Auto-approve when ALL of these are true
options: Order value is below auto-approve limit
Reason is: damaged, wrong item, not received, or defective
Customer account has no previous refund in last 90 days
fields.help: All selected conditions must be true for auto-approval.

fields.label: Auto-approval confirmation message
fields.placeholder: Great news {{name}} — your refund of {{refund_amount}} DZD has been approved! ✅ You'll receive it within {{refund_processing_days}} business days via {{refund_method}}. Is there anything else I can help you with?
fields.help: Variables: {{name}}, {{refund_amount}}, {{refund_processing_days}}, {{refund_method}}, {{order_value}}

fields.label: Default refund method
fields.help: How refunds are issued. Mentioned in the confirmation message.

sections.title: Custom refund rules
sections.description: Define IF/THEN rules that override the default policy for specific cases.
sections.ui: Rule rows. Each rule: IF [condition select] [operator] [value input] THEN [action select]. Add/remove rows. Preview panel shows plain-language summary of all rules.
action_options: Escalate to owner for manual review
Ask for photo proof before deciding
example_rules: IF order_value > 10000 DZD THEN escalate to owner
IF customer_refund_count > 2 in last 90 days THEN escalate to owner
IF delivery_status = lost THEN auto-approve refund
IF reason = not_received AND days_since_delivery < 14 THEN auto-approve
IF product_category = electronics THEN ask for photo proof

sections.title: Refund conversation flow
sections.description: Define the structured steps the agent follows when handling a refund. This is not a rigid script — the LLM handles the actual wording — but these steps guide what the agent asks and in what order.

steps.label: Acknowledge & apologize
steps.description: Agent opens with empathy. Uses opening_message defined above.

steps.label: Identify the issue
steps.description: Agent asks one question to understand the problem. Customer selects or describes a reason.

steps.label: Collect proof (if required)
steps.description: If require_proof is on and reason matches, agent asks for photo.

steps.label: Policy check
steps.description: Internal: agent checks rules. Customer sees nothing during this step.

steps.label: Decision
steps.description: Auto-approve → send confirmation. Escalate → send hold message, notify owner/agent.

steps.label: Close
steps.description: If approved: confirm and ask if anything else needed. If escalated: reassure timeline.

hold_message_field.label: Escalation hold message
hold_message_field.placeholder: Thank you {{name}}, I've passed this to our manager who will personally review your case and get back to you within {{owner_response_sla}}. We take this seriously. 🙏

additional_fields.label: Owner response time commitment
additional_fields.help: Mentioned in the hold message sent to the customer.

sections.title: Triggers
sections.description: When the Refund Agent activates.

fields.label: Agent activates when
options: Order Tracking Agent detects customer dissatisfaction
Customer sends a refund/return keyword in any conversation
Assigned agent manually triggers from contact record
Customer replies with dissatisfaction to a delivery update message

fields.label: Refund trigger keywords
fields.help: Any of these in a customer message will trigger the Refund Agent, regardless of current conversation state.

fields.label: When Refund Agent activates
options: Pause Follow-Up Agent for this contact
Pause Chat Agent for this contact
Pause Order Tracking Agent for this contact
default: Pause Follow-Up Agent for this contact
Pause Chat Agent for this contact

sections.title: Owner notifications
sections.description: Every refund case that is escalated (not auto-approved) triggers a notification to the business owner for manual review and discussion. This is a core feature — the owner is always in the loop on non-trivial refunds.

fields.label: Notify owner on every refund request
fields.help: If off, owner is only notified on escalations (not auto-approved cases).

fields.label: Owner notification recipients
fields.help: These users receive the owner-level notification. Typically the Business Owner and Admin.

fields.label: Owner notification message
fields.help: Variables: {{contact_name}}, {{tracking_code}}, {{order_value}}, {{refund_reason}}, {{days_since_delivery}}, {{conversation_link}}, {{owner_response_sla}}, {{agent_recommendation}}

fields.label: Actions owner can take from notification
fields.content: In-app notification includes: 'Approve refund' button, 'Deny' button, 'Open conversation' link. Clicking Approve/Deny sends the decision back to the Refund Agent, which then messages the customer automatically.

fields.label: Message sent to customer after owner approves
fields.placeholder: Great news {{name}}! Our manager has reviewed your case and approved your refund of {{refund_amount}} DZD. You'll receive it within {{refund_processing_days}} business days. Thank you for your patience! 🙏

fields.label: Message sent to customer after owner denies
fields.placeholder: Hi {{name}}, after reviewing your case, we're unable to process a refund at this time because {{denial_reason}}. We're sorry for the inconvenience. If you'd like to discuss this further, reply here and we'll be happy to help.

fields.label: Also notify assigned sales agent
fields.help: Agent gets a softer notification to stay aware of the case, without requiring their action.

sections.title: Human takeover
sections.description: Full human takeover — agent or owner takes direct control of the refund conversation.

fields.label: Auto-trigger human takeover when
options: Customer uses aggressive or threatening language
Customer mentions legal action or authority
Refund amount exceeds auto-approve limit and owner hasn't responded in X minutes

fields.label: Escalate to direct agent if owner doesn't respond in
fields.help: If the owner doesn't approve/deny within this time, the assigned sales agent is notified to handle it directly.

fields.label: Message when handing to human
fields.placeholder: I've flagged your case as urgent {{name}}. A team member will personally reach out to you within the next 30 minutes. We sincerely apologize for this situation.

fields.label: Play sound for urgent notifications
fields.help: In-app urgent notifications play an alert sound for agents online.

notifications_system.description: Central notification rules page. All escalation alerts, owner notifications, and human intervention triggers are configured and reviewed here. Also shows a live log of all notifications sent.

sections.title: Notification rules
sections.description: Summary table of all notification rules across all agents. Each row: agent name, trigger event, recipients, channels, edit link. Read-only summary — editing redirects to the agent's own configuration page.

sections.title: Notification log
sections.description: Searchable, filterable log of all notifications sent. Columns: timestamp, agent, trigger, recipient, channel, status (Sent / Read / Acted on). Filter by agent, date range, status.

sections.title: Test a notification
sections.description: Select an agent, select a trigger event, pick a recipient, send a test notification. Used to verify delivery before going live.

shared_components_to_build.name: AgentConfigShell
shared_components_to_build.description: Wrapper for all agent config pages. Renders: breadcrumb (AI Agents / [Agent Name]), left anchor nav, center scrollable content, right sticky panel. Props: agentName, anchorSections[], rightPanelContent.

shared_components_to_build.name: LLMConfigSection
shared_components_to_build.description: Reusable LLM config section used by all 4 agents. Props: agentId (used to namespace stored keys). Includes: provider select, API key input with test button, model select, temperature slider, max tokens input.

shared_components_to_build.name: SequenceStepCard
shared_components_to_build.description: Draggable step card for the follow-up sequence builder. Props: stepNumber, onRemove, fields (delay, message mode, template picker, channel selector, send window, stop conditions).

shared_components_to_build.name: TriggerCheckboxGroup
shared_components_to_build.description: Reusable labeled checkbox group with optional condition fields that appear when certain options are selected.

shared_components_to_build.name: EscalationConfig
shared_components_to_build.description: Reusable human handoff configuration block. Props: escalationOptions[], userList[], notificationChannels[]. Renders: escalation trigger checkboxes, user multi-select, channel checkboxes, notification message textarea.

shared_components_to_build.name: RuleBuilder
shared_components_to_build.description: IF/THEN rule builder. Each row: condition dropdown + operator select + value input + action dropdown + remove button. 'Add rule' button. Below: plain-language rule summary list.

shared_components_to_build.name: StatusMappingTable
shared_components_to_build.description: Two-column table: API status (readonly) + customer message (editable textarea) + enabled toggle. Used by Order Tracking Agent.

shared_components_to_build.name: RadioCards
shared_components_to_build.description: Card-style radio buttons. Each option is a bordered card with label + desc. Selected state: border-color #2B62E8, bg #EEF3FD. Unselected: border #E4E4E8, bg white.

shared_components_to_build.name: PasswordInput
shared_components_to_build.description: Input with show/hide toggle and optional 'Test connection' button that runs a mock async check and shows inline success/error.

shared_components_to_build.name: TagInput
shared_components_to_build.description: Type text + Enter to add tags. Each tag shown as a small pill with remove X. Props: placeholder, examples (shown as ghost suggestions below).

shared_components_to_build.name: InlineDuration
shared_components_to_build.description: Two inputs inline: number input + unit select (minutes/hours/days). Returns { value, unit }.

shared_components_to_build.name: TimeRange
shared_components_to_build.description: Two time inputs inline with a dash between: from HH:MM – to HH:MM.

shared_components_to_build.name: Slider
shared_components_to_build.description: HTML range input styled cleanly: 4px track, accent fill left of thumb, 16px thumb circle. Shows current value label. Props: min, max, step, labels (for custom left/right/center labels).

shared_components_to_build.name: InfoBlock
shared_components_to_build.description: Soft informational callout. bg #EEF3FD, border-left 3px #2B62E8, border-radius 4px, padding 12px 16px. Text 13px #1E3A8A. No icon — just text.

shared_components_to_build.name: NotificationBadge
shared_components_to_build.description: Urgent: red background (#DC2626), white text. Normal: bg #EEF3FD, text #1E3A8A. Small pill shown on sidebar nav items and bell icon. No animation — just a number.

shared_components_to_build.name: AgentStatusPanel
shared_components_to_build.description: Right sticky panel for all agent config pages. Shows: enable/disable toggle with status label, 3 live stat rows (label + value), last edited timestamp, 'Test agent' button (outlined secondary).

additions: agentConfigs: object with default configuration for all 4 agents — pre-filled with realistic values so the UI is never empty
refundCases: 8 cases (4 auto-approved, 2 pending owner, 1 denied, 1 in progress)
deliveryStatuses: realistic Yalidine-style status codes with 15 sample orders in various states
notificationLog: 20 notification events across all agents, last 7 days
faqEntries: 12 realistic FAQ pairs for an Algerian e-commerce business
sequenceSteps: default 4-step follow-up sequence with realistic messages in French

implementation_notes: Each agent config page saves state to localStorage keyed by agentId — no backend needed for MVP. Show a save confirmation inline ('Changes saved') without a toast that disappears.
The 'Test connection' button for API keys should show a spinner for 1.5 seconds then resolve to a mocked success state. No actual API call needed for MVP.
The 'Test agent' / 'Test chat' modals should use the actual LLM via the API key saved in config. If no key is set, show 'Add an API key to test this agent'.
Anchor navigation on left: clicking a section label smoothly scrolls to that section. Active section highlighted based on scroll position.

The Refund Agent's owner notification actions ('Approve' / 'Deny' buttons in the in-app notification) should update the refund case status and trigger the appropriate message send. Mock this with state.
Rule Builder preview: below the rule rows, show a plain-English summary: 'If order value exceeds 10,000 DZD → escalate to owner for review.' Update live as rules change.
Status mapping table in Order Tracking: rows for all 8 default statuses pre-populated. Rows with triggers_escalation = true should have a small warning badge on the row.
Notification log should be searchable by contact name and filterable by agent and date.
All text areas with char_counter = true show a live character count bottom-right in 11px #9999AA.
Multi-select users field: shows initials avatar chips for selected users. Clicking removes them. Search input to add more.
Forbidden topics tag input: when a tag is added, show it as a small pill with a red left border to signal its restrictive purpose.
The sequence step drag handle: show as 6 dots icon (⠿) on the left of each step card. Hover: cursor grab. Keep drag-and-drop simple using HTML5 drag events, no library needed.

For the satisfaction check in Order Tracking: show a mini preview of what the delivery-to-satisfaction flow looks like — a static visual (3 boxes connected by arrows: Delivery confirmed → Satisfaction check sent → Customer replies → unsatisfied branch → Refund Agent triggered). Pure HTML/CSS, no library.
Never use the word 'AI' in user-facing UI labels. Use 'agent', 'automation', or the specific agent name. The tech is invisible.
